reasoning: Tesla's current robotaxi deployments in Austin and San Francisco require human safety monitors in the vehicle who remain ready to intervene. Furthermore, during Tesla's Q3 2025 earnings call on October 22-23, Elon Musk explicitly stated that the company expects to remove safety drivers from these vehicles "by the end of this year" or "within a few months," taking a intentionally cautious approach. Given this confirmation just days before the October 31 deadline, it is virtually impossible that Tesla will launch a truly unsupervised system that meets the resolution criteria by the deadline. The market was correctly priced at ~1% prior to this call, and the earnings call unequivocally closed the door on an October launch.
